name: Git Workflow Best Practices
description: Guidelines for effective Git usage, commit messages, and branching strategies
author: Void Team
instructions:
When working with Git:
1. Write clear, concise commit messages following conventional commits format
2. Use feature branches for new work (feature/description)
3. Keep commits atomic - one logical change per commit
4. Rebase instead of merge when updating feature branches
5. Use .gitignore to exclude unnecessary files
6. Review changes before committing (git diff)
7. Use meaningful branch names that describe the purpose
8. Squash commits before merging to main/master
9. Always pull latest changes before pushing
10. Use git stash for temporary work-in-progress
examples: fix: resolve null pointer exception in UserService
docs: update API documentation for v2 endpoints
test: add unit tests for payment processing
